rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Orders Granting Plaintiffs April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Application Effectively Denying The Borrowers Statutory Right Of Redemption As Void And Contrary To Law
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDERS GRANTING PLAINTIFFS APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE APPLICATION EFFECTIVELY DENYING THE BORROWERS STATUTORY RIGHT OF REDEMPTION AS VOID AND CONTRARY TO LAW is DENIED. Nothing of the face of the "judgment roll" indicates that April 22, 2019 order is void. The order concerned an unauthorized encumbrance in violation of a Court order; determination regarding stay requirements on appeal and authorization to obtain counsel. The order did not make determinations regarding right of redemption which is purportedly the basis for the moving party's argument that the order is void. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Order On Grounds That The Motion Was Procedurally Defective Resulting In A Void Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE ORDER ON GROUNDS THAT THE MOTION WAS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE RESULTING IN A VOID ORDER is DENIED. There is nothing on the face of the "judgment roll" indicating that the judgment is void. On April 19, 2019 the ex-parte application was submitted for filing. The ex-parte calendar starts at 9:00 a.m. in the morning and parties are required to provide courtesy copies of their filings in support and in opposition (if any) to the Court. The Court hears the applications and reviews the courtesy copies. Apparently also on April 22, 2019 a clerk of the Court reviewed and rejected the entirety of the filing due to formatting issues because a cover page was missing on the exhibits submitted separately for filing from the application itself. The formatting error was rectified in the re-submitted application and it appears on file with a May 7, 2019 filing date. There is no dispute that the application was submitted for filing and copies were provided to the Court and the parties. All other arguments brought in this motion sound like a motion for reconsideration and do not support finding of voidness (e.g. "the key declarations, were riddled with evidentiary insufficiencies, as the declarations were conclusory, based upon hearsay, and layered with suppositions."). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MAURICIO MAGANA CASTILLO VS. AARON GRUVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike Cross- Claims One And Three
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2021 line 7. PLAINTIFF MAURICIO MAGANA CASTILLO'S NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-CLAIMS ONE AND THREE is GRANTED. Moving party satisfied its burden under the first prong of the anti-slapp. Cross-Complaint asserts claims for Slander of Title and Abuse of Process arising out of protected activity. ("Cross-Defendant filed the Complaint on June 9, 2021, thereafter recorded notices of pendency of action upon Unit 202 and Unit 303, claiming some interest in each of the Subject Properties, and casting doubt upon Cross-Complainant's ownership."). Opposing party failed to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits re: privileged communications. CC 47(b)(4). Lis pendens identifed an action "previously filed" in a court of competent jurisdiction that affects title or right to possession of real property, i.e. a claim in a pleading which would, if meritorious, affect title to real property. Alpha & Omega Dev., LP v. Whillock Contracting, Inc. (2012) 200 Cal.App.4th 656. The evidentiary support or merits of such claim are irrelevant to this analysis. Additionally, recordation of lis pendens and filing/maintenance of a lawsuit cannot support a cause of action for abuse of process. Woodcourt II Limited v. McDonald Co. (1981) 119 Cal.App.3d 245; JSJ Limited Partnership v. Mehrban (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1512. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: POSEE CHUNG VS. RICK QURESHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 12, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANT RICK QURESHI'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO VACATE VOID JUDGMENT is DENIED. This Court's April 7, 2021 issued after bifurcated bench trial is currently on appeal according to this Court's register of action, i.e. no remittitur on file. The issue of standing is necessarily decided by the trial Court and in this action the issue of ownership has been brought up and was decided by the trial judge in both phases of trial. See Order on Motion for Summary Judgment. This issue is currently before the Court of Appeal leaving this Court without jurisdiction to address it. Had this issue not been on appeal, the motion would be denied as the moving party failed to establish the judgment is void. "[A] revocable inter vivos trust is recognized as simply 'a probate avoidance device,'" and "when property is held in this type of trust, the settlor and lifetime beneficiary ' "has the equivalent of full ownership of the property." ' (Zanelli v. McGrath (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 615, 633-634, 82 Cal.Rptr.3d 835; Steinhart v. County of Los Angeles, supra, 47 Cal.4th at p. 1320, 104 Cal.Rptr.3d 195, 223 P.3d 57.) Boshernitsan v. Bach (2021) 61 Cal.App.5th 883, 891-892. Defendant requests judicial notice of a Quit Claim Deed transferring the property into a revocable living trust. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
